description: Returns the value of a browser cookie by name. Use for User ID tracking without re-authentication, visit counts (e.g. first-time vs returning visitor), or any value your site stores in a cookie. Result is usually a string; numeric strings are converted to numbers and TRUE/FALSE strings to booleans.

when_to_use: Extend User ID tracking for return users without requiring re-authentication
Store and read visit count (e.g. fire different tags for first-time vs x-time visitor)
Read consent or preference values stored in cookies
Use session or persistent identifiers set by your site or auth system
Pass values between pages via cookies for tags and triggers
common_use_cases: User ID: read auth/session cookie for consistent user identification
Visit count: cookie holding number of visits for segmenting first-time vs returning
Consent state: cookie set by CMP or your app
Campaign/source: UTM or referral stored in a cookie
A/B test variant: variant ID stored in cookie
best_practices: Use the exact cookie name as set by your site or application
For complex values (e.g. JSON), handle parsing in a custom JavaScript variable if needed
Respect cookie lifetime and domain – variable returns empty if cookie is missing or expired
Document which cookie names your tags depend on for team and debugging

create_variable.description: Create a cookie variable that returns the value of the specified cookie

value.description: Name of the cookie to read (API: value). At runtime the variable returns that cookie's value.
Name of the cookie to read (API: value). The variable's value at runtime is the cookie's content.
value.recommendation: Use the exact cookie name as set by your site. Cookie names are case-sensitive.

create_example.description: Create a cookie variable for session ID

update_example.description: Update the cookie name for an existing cookie variable

visit_count_example.description: Cookie variable for visit count (e.g. first-time vs returning)

common_mistakes: Using the cookie's content as value – value must be the cookie name to read
Typos or wrong case in cookie name – cookie names are case-sensitive
Assuming the cookie is always set – variable can be empty if cookie is missing or expired
Expecting complex (e.g. JSON) cookie content without parsing – use custom JS variable if needed

variable_empty_or_undefined.problem: Cookie variable returns empty or undefined
solutions: Confirm the cookie is set (check Application > Cookies in dev tools)
Verify value (cookie name) matches exactly (case and spelling)
Check cookie domain and path – variable runs in page context where cookie is visible
Ensure cookie exists at the time the variable is evaluated (e.g. after login)

wrong_type_from_cookie.problem: Need number or boolean but getting string
solutions: Numeric strings and TRUE/FALSE are auto-converted; for other formats use a custom JavaScript variable to parse the cookie value
